## List Application Fees

`<stripe:get-v1-application-fees>`

Returns a list of application fees you'Âve previously collected. The application fees are returned in
sorted order, with the most recent fees appearing first. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to
the /v1/application_fees endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>expand</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>charge</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return application fees for the charge specified by this charge ID.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A limit on the number of objects to be returned. ## Get Application Fee

`<stripe:get-v1-application-fees-by-id>`

Retrieves the details of an application fee that your account has collected. The same information is
returned when refunding the application fee. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/application_fees/{id} endpoint

## Get Refund For Application Fee

operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/application_fees/{fee}/refunds/{id} endpoint

## List Refunds For Application Fee

`<stripe:get-v1-application-fees-refunds-by-id>`

You can see a list of the refunds belonging to a specific application fee. Note that the 10 most
recent refunds are always available by default on the application fee object. If you need more than
those 10, you can use this API method and the limit and starting_after parameters to page through
additional refunds. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/application_fees/{id}/refunds endpoint

### Parameters

## Get Balance

`<stripe:get-v1-balance>`

Retrieves the current account balance, based on the authentication that was used to make the
request. For a sample request, see Accounting for negative balances. This operation makes an HTTP
GET request to the /v1/balance endpoint

## Get Balance History

`<stripe:get-v1-balance-history>`

Returns a list of transactions that have contributed to the Stripe account balance (, for example,
charges, transfers, and so forth). The transactions are returned in sorted order, with the most
recent transactions appearing first. Note that this endpoint was previously called 'ÂBalance history'Â
and used the path /v1/balance/history. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/balance/history endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ending before</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A cursor for use in pagination. <code>ending_before</code> is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, starting with <code>obj_bar</code>, your subsequent call can include <code>ending_before=obj_bar</code> to fetch the previous page of the list.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Available on</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Available on</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>source</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only returns the original transaction.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>payout</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>For automatic Stripe payouts only, only returns transactions that were paid out on the specified payout ID.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Starting after</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A cursor for use in pagination. <code>starting_after</code> is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, ending with <code>obj_foo</code>, your subsequent call can include <code>starting_after=obj_foo</code> to fetch the next page of the list.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>expand</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>currency</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return transactions in a certain currency. Three-letter [ISO currency code](<a href="https://www.iso.org/iso-4217-currency-codes.html">https://www.iso.org/iso-4217-currency-codes.html</a>), in lowercase. Must be a [supported currency](<a href="https://docs.stripe.com/currencies">https://docs.stripe.com/currencies</a>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only returns transactions of the given type. One of: <code>charge</code>, <code>refund</code>, <code>adjustment</code>, <code>application_fee</code>, <code>application_fee_refund</code>, <code>transfer</code>, <code>payment</code>, <code>payout</code>, <code>payout_failure</code>, <code>stripe_fee</code>, or <code>network_cost</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A limit on the number of objects to be returned. ## List Balance Transactions

`<stripe:get-v1-balance-transactions>`

Returns a list of transactions that have contributed to the Stripe account balance (, for example,
charges, transfers, and so forth). The transactions are returned in sorted order, with the most
recent transactions appearing first. Note that this endpoint was previously called 'ÂBalance history'Â
and used the path /v1/balance/history. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/balance_transactions endpoint

## List all charges

`<stripe:get-v1-charges>`

Returns a list of charges you'Âve previously created. The charges are returned in sorted order, with
the most recent charges appearing first. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/charges
endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>expand</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>customer</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return charges for the customer specified by this customer ID.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Transfer group</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return charges for this transfer group.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>created[gt]</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>created[gt]</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 10.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Payment intent</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return charges that were created by the PaymentIntent specified by this PaymentIntent ID.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>created</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>created</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit which qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Retrieve a charge

`<stripe:get-v1-charges-by-charge>`

Retrieves the details of a charge that has previously been created. Supply the unique charge ID that
was returned from your previous request, and Stripe will return the corresponding charge
information. The same information is returned when creating or refunding the charge. This operation
makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/charges/{charge} endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>charge</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>charge</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>expand</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit which qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## List Charge Refunds

`<stripe:get-v1-charges-refunds-by-charge>`

You can see a list of the refunds belonging to a specific charge. Note that the 10 most recent
refunds are always available by default on the charge object. If you need more than those 10, you
can use this API method and the limit and starting_after parameters to page through additional
refunds. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/charges/{charge}/refunds endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>charge</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>charge</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>expand</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 10.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. ## List all Checkout Sessions

`<stripe:get-v1-checkout-sessions>`

Returns a list of Checkout Sessions. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/checkout/sessions endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>subscription</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return the Checkout Session for the subscription specified.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>expand</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. /v1/coupons/{coupon} endpoint

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>coupon</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>coupon</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>expand</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. endpoint

## Get Credit Note Preview

`<stripe:get-v1-credit-notes-preview>`

Get a preview of a credit note without creating it. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/credit_notes/preview endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Refund amount</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The integer amount in <strong>%s</strong> representing the amount to refund. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit which qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. ## Get Credit Note Preview Lines

`<stripe:get-v1-credit-notes-preview-lines>`

When retrieving a credit note preview, you'Âll get a lines property containing the first handful of
those items. This URL you can retrieve the full (paginated) list of line items. This operation makes
an HTTP GET request to the /v1/credit_notes/preview/lines endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ending before</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A cursor for use in pagination. <code>ending_before</code> is an object ID that defines your place in the list. ## List all customers

`<stripe:get-v1-customers>`

Returns a list of your customers. The customers are returned sorted by creation date, with the most
recent customers appearing first. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/customers
endpoint

### Parameters

The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Retrieve a customer

`<stripe:get-v1-customers-by-customer>`

Retrieves the details of an existing customer. You need only supply the unique customer identifier
that was returned upon customer creation. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/customers/{customer} endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>customer</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>customer</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>expand</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. ## List customer active subscriptions

`<stripe:get-v1-customers-subscriptions-by-customer>`

You can see a list of the customer active subscriptions. Note that the 10 most recent active
subscriptions are always available by default on the customer object. If you need more than those
10, you can use the limit and starting_after parameters to page through additional subscriptions.
This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/customers/{customer}/subscriptions endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>customer</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>customer</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>expand</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 10.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit which qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. ## List all disputes

`<stripe:get-v1-disputes>`

Returns a list of your disputes. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/disputes
endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>expand</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>charge</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return disputes associated to the charge specified by this charge ID.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 10.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Payment intent</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return disputes associated to the PaymentIntent specified by this PaymentIntent ID.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>created</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>created</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit which qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## List all events

`<stripe:get-v1-events>`

List events, going back up to 30 days. Each event data is rendered according to Stripe API version
at its creation time, specified in event object api_version attribute (not according to your current
Stripe API version or Stripe-Version header). This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/events endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>expand</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A string containing a specific event name, or group of events using * as a wildcard. The list will be filtered to include only events with a matching event property.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>created[gt]</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>created[gt]</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>types</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An array of up to 20 strings containing specific event names. The list will be filtered to include only events with a matching event property. You may pass either <code>type</code> or <code>types</code>, but not both.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A limit on the number of objects to be returned. ## List all exchange rates

`<stripe:get-v1-exchange-rates>`

Returns a list of objects that contain the rates at which foreign currencies are converted to one
another. Only shows the currencies for which Stripe supports. This operation makes an HTTP GET
request to the /v1/exchange_rates endpoint

### Parameters

## Retrieve and exchange rate

`<stripe:get-v1-exchange-rates-by-currency>`

Retrieves the exchange rates from the given currency to every supported currency. This operation
makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/exchange_rates/{currency} endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>currency</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>currency</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>expand</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit which qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## List all files

`<stripe:get-v1-files>`

Returns a list of the files that your account has access to. The files are returned sorted by
creation date, with the most recently created files appearing first. This operation makes an HTTP
GET request to the /v1/files endpoint

### Parameters

## Retrieve a file

operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/files/{file} endpoint

## List all invoice items

`<stripe:get-v1-invoiceitems>`

Returns a list of your invoice items. Invoice items are returned sorted by creation date, with the
most recently created invoice items appearing first. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/invoiceitems endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>expand</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>invoice</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return invoice items belonging to this invoice. If none is provided, all invoice items will be returned. If specifying an invoice, no customer identifier is needed.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>customer</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The identifier of the customer whose invoice items to return. If none is provided, all invoice items will be returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 10.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>pending</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Set to <code>true</code> to only show pending invoice items, which are not yet attached to any invoices. Set to <code>false</code> to only show invoice items already attached to invoices. If unspecified, no filter is applied.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>created</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>created</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit which qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. ## List all invoices

`<stripe:get-v1-invoices>`

You can list all invoices, or list the invoices for a specific customer. The invoices are returned
sorted by creation date, with the most recently created invoices appearing first. This operation
makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/invoices endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Collection method</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The collection method of the invoice to retrieve. ## Retrieve an invoice''s line items

`<stripe:get-v1-invoices-lines-by-invoice>`

When retrieving an invoice, you'Âll get a lines property containing the total count of line items and
the first handful of those items. There is also a URL where you can retrieve the full (paginated)
list of line items. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/invoices/{invoice}/lines
endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>invoice</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>invoice</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>expand</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ending before</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A cursor for use in pagination. <code>ending_before</code> is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, starting with <code>obj_bar</code>, your subsequent call can include <code>ending_before=obj_bar</code> to fetch the previous page of the list.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 10.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Starting after</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A cursor for use in pagination. <code>starting_after</code> is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, ending with <code>obj_foo</code>, your subsequent call can include <code>starting_after=obj_foo</code> to fetch the next page of the list.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit which qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Retrieve an upcoming invoice

`<stripe:get-v1-invoices-upcoming>`

At any time, you can preview the upcoming invoice for a customer. This will show you all the charges
that are pending, including subscription renewal charges, invoice item charges, etc. It will also
show you any discount that is applicable to the customer. Note that when you are viewing an upcoming
invoice, you are simply viewing a preview - the invoice has not yet been created. As such, the
upcoming invoice will not show up in invoice listing calls, and you cannot use the API to pay or
edit the invoice. If you want to change the amount that your customer will be billed, you can add,
remove, or update pending invoice items, or update the customerâs discount. You can preview the
effects of updating a subscription, including a preview of what proration will take place. To ensure
that the actual proration is calculated exactly the same as the previewed proration, you should pass
a proration_date parameter when doing the actual subscription update. The value passed in should be
the same as the subscription_proration_date returned on the upcoming invoice resource. The
recommended way to get only the prorations being previewed is to consider only proration line items
where period\[start\] is equal to the subscription_proration_date on the upcoming invoice resource.
This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/invoices/upcoming endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Subscription tax percent</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>If provided, the invoice returned will preview updating or creating a subscription with that tax percent. If set, one of <code>subscription_items</code> or <code>subscription</code> is required. This field has been deprecated and will be removed in a future API version, for further information view the [migration docs](<a href="https://docs.stripe.com/billing/taxes/tax-rates">https://docs.stripe.com/billing/taxes/tax-rates</a>) for <code>tax_rates</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>subscription</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The identifier of the subscription for which you''d like to retrieve the upcoming invoice. If not provided, but a <code>subscription_items</code> is provided, you will preview creating a subscription with those items. If neither <code>subscription</code> nor <code>subscription_items</code> is provided, you will retrieve the next upcoming invoice from among the customer''s subscriptions.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Subscription proration date</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>If previewing an update to a subscription, and doing proration, <code>subscription_proration_date</code> forces the proration to be calculated as though the update was done at the specified time. The time given must be within the current subscription period, and cannot be before the subscription was on its current plan. If set, <code>subscription</code>, and one of <code>subscription_items</code>, or <code>subscription_trial_end</code> are required. Also, <code>subscription_proration</code> cannot be set to false.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>coupon</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The code of the coupon to apply. If <code>subscription</code> or <code>subscription_items</code> is provided, the invoice returned will preview updating or creating a subscription with that coupon. Otherwise, it will preview applying that coupon to the customer for the next upcoming invoice from among the customer''s subscriptions. The invoice can be previewed without a coupon by passing this value as an empty string.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Subscription cancel now</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>This simulates the subscription being canceled or expired immediately.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Subscription default tax rates</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>If provided, the invoice returned will preview updating or creating a subscription with these default tax rates. The default tax rates will apply to any line item that does not have <code>tax_rates</code> set.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Subscription trial from plan</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates if a plan''s <code>trial_period_days</code> should be applied to the subscription. Setting <code>subscription_trial_end</code> per subscription is preferred, and this defaults to <code>false</code>. Setting this flag to <code>true</code> together with <code>subscription_trial_end</code> is not allowed.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Subscription trial end</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>If provided, the invoice returned will preview updating or creating a subscription with that trial end. If set, one of <code>subscription_items</code> or <code>subscription</code> is required.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>expand</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Invoice items</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>List of invoice items to add or update in the upcoming invoice preview.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Subscription proration behavior</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Determines how to handle [prorations](<a href="https://docs.stripe.com/billing/subscriptions/billing-cycle">https://docs.stripe.com/billing/subscriptions/billing-cycle</a>) when the billing cycle changes (, for example, when switching plans, resetting <code>billing_cycle_anchor=now</code>, or starting a trial), or if an itemâs <code>quantity</code> changes. Passing <code>create_prorations</code> will cause proration invoice items to be created when applicable. Cannot be used with subscription or subscription fields.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Subscription prorate</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>This field has been renamed to <code>subscription_proration_behavior</code>. <code>subscription_prorate=true</code> can be replaced with <code>subscription_proration_behavior=create_prorations</code> and <code>subscription_prorate=false</code> can be replaced with <code>subscription_proration_behavior=none</code>.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Subscription start date</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Date a subscription is intended to start (can be future or past)</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Subscription billing cycle anchor</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>For new subscriptions, a future timestamp to anchor the subscription''s [billing cycle](<a href="https://docs.stripe.com/billing/subscriptions/billing-cycle">https://docs.stripe.com/billing/subscriptions/billing-cycle</a>). ## Retrieve an upcoming invoice''s line items

`<stripe:get-v1-invoices-upcoming-lines>`

When retrieving an upcoming invoice, you'Âll get a lines property containing the total count of line
items and the first handful of those items. There is also a URL where you can retrieve the full
(paginated) list of line items. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/invoices/upcoming/lines endpoint

Will prorate if within the current period and prorations have been enabled using <code>proration_behavior</code>.`</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit which qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## List all authorizations

`<stripe:get-v1-issuing-authorizations>`

Returns a list of Issuing Authorization objects. The objects are sorted in descending order by
creation date, with the most recently created object appearing first. This operation makes an HTTP
GET request to the /v1/issuing/authorizations endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>expand</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>status</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return authorizations with the given status. One of <code>pending</code>, <code>closed</code>, or <code>reversed</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>card</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return issuing transactions that belong to the given card.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 10.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>created</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return authorizations that were created during the given date interval.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>cardholder</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return authorizations belonging to the given cardholder.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. the /v1/issuing/cards endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Exp month</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return cards that have the given expiration month.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Exp year</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return cards that have the given expiration year.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>expand</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>last4</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return cards that have the given last four digits.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>status</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return cards that have the given status. One of <code>active</code>, <code>inactive</code>, or <code>canceled</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return cards that have the given type. One of <code>virtual</code> or <code>physical</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 10.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>created</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return cards that were issued during the given date interval.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>cardholder</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return cards belonging to the Cardholder with the provided ID.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit which qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

/v1/issuing/settlements/{settlement} endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>settlement</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>settlement</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>expand</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit which qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## List all transactions

`<stripe:get-v1-issuing-transactions>`

Returns a list of Issuing Transaction objects. The objects are sorted in descending order by
creation date, with the most recently created object appearing first. This operation makes an HTTP
GET request to the /v1/issuing/transactions endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>expand</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>card</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return transactions that belong to the given card.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 10.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>created</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return transactions that were created during the given date interval.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>cardholder</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return transactions that belong to the given cardholder.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. ## List all order returns

`<stripe:get-v1-order-returns>`

Returns a list of your order returns. The returns are returned sorted by creation date, with the
most recently created return appearing first. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/order_returns endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>expand</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>order</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The order to retrieve returns for.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A limit on the number of objects to be returned. ## Retrieve an order return

`<stripe:get-v1-order-returns-by-id>`

Retrieves the details of an existing order return. Supply the unique order ID from either an order
return creation request or the order return list, and Stripe will return the corresponding order
information. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/order_returns/{id} endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>expand</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit which qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## List all orders

`<stripe:get-v1-orders>`

Returns a list of your orders. The orders are returned sorted by creation date, with the most
recently created orders appearing first. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/orders
endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Status transitions</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Filter orders based on when they were paid, fulfilled, canceled, or returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>expand</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>status</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return orders that have the given status. One of <code>created</code>, <code>paid</code>, <code>fulfilled</code>, or <code>refunded</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>customer</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return orders for the given customer.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return orders with the given IDs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>created[gt]</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>created[gt]</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 10.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Upstream ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return orders with the given upstream order IDs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>created</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Date this order was created.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit which qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming.
